title: Odes Of Anacreon - Ode XXVII.
content:
We read the flying courser's name
    Upon his side, in marks of flame;
    And, by their turbaned brows alone,
    The warriors of the East are known.
    But in the lover's glowing eyes,
    The inlet to his bosom lies;
    Through them we see the small faint mark,
    Where Love has dropt his burning spark!
meaning:
"Odes Of Anacreon - Ode XXVII." is a quintessential example of Thomas Moore's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
